description: Shared workflow policy for Pi and Claude adapters. Runtime adapters may keep their own storage, UI, and tool-gate implementations, but phase order, transition policy, reminder intent, and context-management rules should stay aligned with this file.

hardRules: Never skip phases; only advance to the next phase.
The only user-approval boundary is commit → push. Everything from interview through commit is autonomous.
plan ↔ plan_review is a repair loop: DPAA fail → auto-return to plan → fix → workflow_approve → plan_review → repeat until DPAA passes.
implement ↔ code_review is a repair loop: quality/review fail → workflow_state prev → implement → fix → workflow_approve → code_review → repeat until review passes.
Use subagents for implementation, review, large diffs, and logs.
Keep main context to decisions, changed files, verification, blockers, next phase.

phaseGuidance.interview: Clarify requirements only; write interview artifacts before planning.
phaseGuidance.plan: Write the implementation plan; do not modify production code yet.
phaseGuidance.plan_review: Review the plan and run DPAA. Auto-advances to implement on DPAA pass; auto-returns to plan on fail.
phaseGuidance.implement: Implement only the approved plan; prefer subagents for context-heavy implementation work.
phaseGuidance.code_review: Repair loop: run self-review, independent review, and quality gate. If quality fails or Critical/Major found, call workflow_state prev to return to implement, fix, then workflow_approve back to code_review. Repeat autonomously until review passes (Critical=0, Major≤2), then submit_review_package.
phaseGuidance.commit: Present verification, risk, diff summary, and proposed commit message before push approval.
phaseGuidance.push: Push only after policy checks and explicit push-phase approval.
phaseGuidance.done: Workflow is complete; start a new workflow for new changes.
